tokens.description: Doxycycline.  FDA-approved antibiotic, demonstrated to extend median lifespan in worms by greater than 90% in multiple studies. At low doses, shown to activate key longevity pathways that enhance mitochondrial function, reduce inflammation, and support cellular waste removal.  Off-label use to treat chronic inflammatory conditions and neurodegenerative disease. Launched by Rapamycin Longevity Lab in collaboration with the Center for Healthy Longevity, National University of Singapore.

This trio delivers a “stop, clear, and upgrade” assault on aging, beginning with Omipalisib’s potent shutdown of the PI3K-mTOR growth axis to prevent new damage. Ginsenoside Rh2 then provides a one-two punch of clearing inflammatory senescent cells while quelling the background inflammation that drives tissue decay. Urolithin A completes the cycle by recycling old, inefficient mitochondria, ensuring the newly protected cells are powered by a clean and robust energy supply.

Omipalisib + Doxycycline. Highly promising longevity cocktail, shown to more than double the lifespan of worms with an unprecedented 109% median lifespan extension.  Potent mTOR inhibitor combined with an FDA-approved antibiotic with dose-dependent anti-inflammatory effects.  Represents a bold bet on synergistic mechanisms to push the boundaries of lifespan extension. Launched by Rapamycin Longevity Lab in collaboration with the Center for Healthy Longevity, National University of Singapore.
Kicking of the next wave of DeSci with some real $ART.

Rapamycin + Doxycycline.  Independently validated for strong lifespan effects (Rapa 37%, Doxy 95%). Both FDA-approved drugs, a well-validated mTOR inhibitor combined with a popular antibiotic and anti-inflammatory.  Highly promising longevity cocktail with a fast path to market, allowing you to invest in longevity interventions today that may fuel your longevity tomorrow. Launched by Rapamycin Longevity Lab in collaboration with the Center for Healthy Longevity, National University of Singapore.
Sulforaphane (B-roccoli sprouts) plus Uridine (yEAST)  . Clears toxins and drives new mitochondria—imagine pruning old smart contracts and upgrading throughput. Bullish for cellular cleanup.

Epicatechin has shown promise in improving vascular function, enhancing mitochondrial biogenesis, and supporting muscle health.

Glycine is essential for collagen production, glutathione synthesis, and metabolic detoxification.

Together, this synergy may enhance mitochondrial resilience, reduce oxidative stress, and delay senescence - making Chronoflex a compelling candidate for anti-aging, muscle preservation, and metabolic support.
Krebs cycle intermediate plus amino acid. Balances energy creation and neurotransmitter boosts—like syncing two validators for reliable consensus. Bullish for stable energy rails.

Urolithin A is a compound that your body makes when you eat foods rich in ellagitannins, like pomegranates. What makes it special is its ability to give your cells a “spring cleaning.” It helps clear out old, dysfunctional mitochondria—the energy factories of the cell—allowing the fresh, healthy ones to thrive. This process, known as mitophagy, has been shown to extend the lifespan of C. elegans by making their cells more efficient and energetic. In human studies, Urolithin A has shown promise in boosting muscle health and energy, which tend to decline as we age. Think of it as a reset button for your cells, helping to keep them functioning at their best. While we’re still uncovering its full potential, Urolithin A is a fascinating link between diet, gut health, and longevity.

Metformin's ability to enhance metabolic efficiency is tantalizing. Its role in activating AMP-activated protein kinase (AMPK) presents it as a fringe candidate for peak recovery. Alas, it’s bound by its diabetic lineage and prescription chains.

RULI uniquely unites Rapamycin, Urolithin A, and Lithium to attack aging on three critical fronts: mTOR inhibition to slow cellular decline, mitophagy activation to renew mitochondrial powerhouses, and neuroprotection via GSK-3β inhibition and FOXO activation. This rare synergy not only extends lifespan across species but preserves energy and cognitive resilience, making it one of the most comprehensive and balanced longevity strategies ever conceived.

Omipalisib is a powerful mTOR and PI3K inhibitor. Originally developed to treat advanced cancers, it has recently been shown to extend the median lifespan in worms by as much as 63%! Unlike Rapamycin, Omipalisib directly inhibits mTORC1 and mTORC2. And even though it robustly boosts autophagy, taking this drug can be risky. But what happens when we combine Omipalisib with SMER28?
As an mTOR-independent autophagy enhancer, SMER28 might allow us to greatly reduce the dose of Omipalisib we need, making it much safer and even more effective.
Epigallocatechin gallate (EGCG) from green tea and sulforaphane (SFN) from cruciferous vegetables are powerful natural compounds known for their potential to promote longevity properties.

The most powerful natural sphingolipid synthesis inhibitor shown to dramatically extend lifespan in C. elegans by up to 70%, outperforming many benchmark longevity compounds.

Originally derived from fungi, Myriocin acts through the mTOR-independent ceramide pathway, making it a unique and underexplored route to lifespan extension across species.

In a world full of repurposed drugs, $MYRIO stands out with bold science, untapped potential, and a mission to defy biological time.
https://x.com/GuntherEagleman/status/1973017881379013032

TCP+Repsox (T+Rex) is the first chemical reprogramming intervention built and published by world-renowned labs to reverse aging, not only slow it down. Showing over 40% lifespan extension in all models tested including C. elegans and mice, plus strong healthspan gains including motor and neurological functions, it is built on decades of research targeting the reversal of aging at its core, epigenetically. Powered by the Reprog Labs x RLL collaboration. The first chemical reprogramming launch.

One of the most promising compounds tested to date is the mTOR inhibitor Rapamycin. Notably, it's the only compound that has consistently demonstrated significant longevity effects across multiple species, including yeast, worms, flies and mice. The efficiency and reliability of reproducing healthy lifespan extension across multiple species has led to that partial mTOR inhibition is viewed as the gold standard in the field.
Extracted from red ginseng. Activates energy switch AMPK and ramps up immunity—like a security patch that fortifies your network against hacks. Bullish for stress resilience.
Green-tea catechin plus gut-made pomegranate metabolite. Primes antioxidant defenses and clears old mitochondria—imagine running a node-cleanup script. Bullish for mitochondrial health.

Melatonin is the most slept on antioxidant supplement available today.  It is approximately 7x more potent than Vitamin C as an antioxidant, and is one of the most abundant and safe antioxidant species found in nature.  Studies have shown melatonin is effective at regulating circadian rhythm and preventing or delaying age-related diseases.  Rick Strassman studied melatonin via intravenous administration and demonstrated no adverse effects in man.

Quick rundown on indoleproprionamide:  >powerful antioxidant  >melatonin derivative (w higher bioavailability)  >trace amounts within every cell of your body  >unknown biochemical origin  >can catch and redirect electrons in the electron transport chain  >increases complex I/complex IV activity  >increases ATP levels, cellular energy currency  >doubles the size of rotifers
From vine tea and citrus peel. Speeds liver detox and widens vessels—like flushing spam tx’s and boosting network bandwidth. Bullish for clean blood flow and toxin removal.

This stack uses Rapamycin (~30% lifespan increase) to hit the master mTOR switch, shifting the cellular economy from growth to repair and initiating broad autophagy. Ginsenoside Rh2 (~24% lifespan increase) complements this by suppressing the chronic, low-grade inflammation that accelerates aging, protecting tissues from systemic damage. Urolithin A (5-20% lifespan increase) adds a final layer of targeted quality control, selectively culling dysfunctional mitochondria to boost energy efficiency

Astaxanthin is a marine antioxidant from the carotenoid family, renowned for its exceptional potency—up to 6,000 times more effective than vitamin C at neutralizing free radicals. 
Produced by microalgae, it protects cell membranes from oxidative stress, improves mitochondrial function, and reduces chronic inflammation—two key drivers of aging.
Studies have shown it can extend lifespan in worms (C. elegans) and improve stress resistance in flies and mammals.
ASTAX = The red shield against aging

Thymoquinone - the compound behind the ‘cure for everything but death’. Known for anti-aging, anti-inflammatory, immune-boosting & more. It’s being researched globally for cancer, heart, brain & metabolic health. With pumpdotscience, there’s no gatekeeping: fully transparent and on-chain. From seed to survival – the future of health is here! We let the science do the talking!

Coenzyme Q10 (CoQ10) is a vital redox-active molecule embedded in the mitochondrial inner membrane, where it serves as an electron carrier in the electron transport chain, enabling efficient ATP synthesis. With age, endogenous CoQ10 synthesis declines, leading to impaired mitochondrial respiration, elevated oxidative stress, and cumulative biomolecular damage. Funding CoQ10 research allows us to better understand and restore mitochondrial bioenergetics, stabilize redox homeostasis, and mitigate.
SMER28 - Rapamycin experiment
NAD⁺ cofactor plus α-ketoglutarate (TCA cycle). Supercharges energy cycles and gene repair—like combining a base asset with a yield booster. Bullish for metabolic throughput.

Silibinin is a flavonolignan isolated from milk thistle, Silybum marianum, that has been shown to exhibit antioxidant and antineoplastic activities. It has a role as an antioxidant, an antineoplastic agent, a hepatoprotective agent and a plant metabolite. It is a flavonolignan, a polyphenol, an aromatic ether, a benzodioxine and a secondary alpha-hydroxy ketone.

Unleash Your Inner Wolverine: Discover BPC-157.  Imagine a compound so potent, so revolutionary, it's earned the nickname "The Wolverine Peptide" for its incredible power to regenerate and heal. This isn't science fiction; it's the reality of BPC-157, and it's set to transform how we approach recovery, repair, and peak physical condition. Lead by og DeSci Innovative Bioresearch.

**Methylene Blue (MB)** is a century-old compound with renewed relevance in longevity research. It enhances mitochondrial efficiency, boosts cellular energy production, and acts as a potent antioxidant, reducing oxidative stress. Studies suggest benefits for cognitive performance, memory, and neuroprotection, as well as resilience against age-related decline—positioning MB as a promising tool in healthy aging and brain health.
Berberine (metabolism regulator) and punicalagin (pomegranate). Optimizes lipids and soaks up free radicals—like hedging against price swings and reducing “gas” fees. Bullish for heart health.

Rilmenidine is an imidazoline receptor agonist with antihypertensive effects, shown to mimic caloric restriction in C. elegans by activating autophagy and stress response pathways. Preclinical studies suggest it may promote longevity and improve metabolic health without dietary changes. its favorable safety profile and oral availability make it a promising candidate for  age-related disease research.
bull trap

Metformin → AMPK activation, mitohormesis
Quercetin → NF-κB inhibition, NRF2 activation
Synergy: Improves glucose control, lowers chronic inflammation, strengthens antioxidant defenses, and supports protein homeostasis.

Rationale: AMPK-driven metabolic tuning plus NF-κB/NRF2 modulation improves glycemic stability and cellular defenses. Novel combo.

Theaflavin is a polyphenolic compound formed during black tea fermentation, responsible for its color and flavor, and known for its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and health-promoting properties. Contains benzotropolone rings formed by condensation of two catechin molecules (like epicatechin and epigallocatechin). phenolic hydroxyl (-OH)
Bupropion is known to modulate dopamine and norepinephrine levels, providing a lift in mood and reducing addictive behavior.

Fisetin and quercetin clear senescent "zombie" cells to curb inflammation; spermidine induces autophagy for debris recycling—in a 2-3 day/month pulse mimicking fasting resets. Preclinical: Fisetin boosts mouse lifespan ~10% via senolysis; spermidine adds 10% through EP300/autophagy (Eisenberg 2009; 2024 Nature). Emerging human pilots show biomarker promise (SmartAge RCT).

Miracil D (also known as Lucanthone or Nilodin) is a 10-thiaxanthenone drug synthesized in 1939 that acts as an oral agent for treating schistosomiasis (parasitic flatworm infection). It also exhibits carcinostatic (tumor-inhibiting) properties in rodents by inhibiting RNA and DNA synthesis.

Praised by Sigmund Freud. Cocaine is a stimulant derived from coca leaves, with the active alkaloid cocaine (C₁₇H₂₁NO₄). Its structure contains carbon, hydrogen, nitrogen, and oxygen atoms. It blocks dopamine, serotonin, and norepinephrine reuptake, creating euphoria and alertness. Despite high addiction risks, cocaine has medical benefits: it is used as a local anesthetic and vasoconstrictor in certain ear, nose, and throat surgeries, where it numbs tissue and reduces bleeding.
Ifosfamide is a well-known chemotherapy agent with established clinical usage and pharmacological properties. Its molecular structure and activity are well-documented in oncology.

BAM15 is an experimental mitochondrial uncoupler that increases cellular energy expenditure by transporting protons across the inner mitochondrial membrane. In C. elegans, it activates longevity pathways (AMPK, PGC-1α, SIRT1), boosting mitochondrial biogenesis, autophagy, and stress resistance. BAM15 extends lifespan (up to +25%) and locomotion, improves insulin sensitivity, reduces inflammation, and mitigates metabolic aging, with far greater safety than DNP.
Known for its calming yet focusing effects, L-Theanine can prepare the mind for a smooth launch.

17alpha-Estradiol is a gentler mirror-image of estradiol. In multiple mouse studies, males lived longer with better metabolic health and minimal feminizing effects. Promising, but human data is early.

For stress and anxiety reduction, Sertraline, Buspirone, and Hydroxyzine offer effective options. Sertraline, a classic SSRI, balances serotonin levels. Buspirone uniquely modulates serotonin receptors, and Hydroxyzine, an antihistamine, provides sedative relief. Each compound requires a prescription and carries a well-documented safety profile. They elegantly address the tendrils of anxiety with precision and scientific integrity.
Potential anti-aging and health benefits, like boosting immunity and improving circulation, with research suggesting it helps clear aged red blood cells and acts as an antioxidant

No introduction needed. this compound cocktail is expected to achieve  over 130% life expectancy in c elegans and dominate king of the pill on pump science. Science Doge is hard at work to deliver the best performing compounds and find the best longevity compound on desci blockchain. future profits from affiliate product sales once human testing is complete will be used for token buybacks

Though not directly linked to lifespan extension, its roles in cellular repair and anti-inflammatory processes suggest potential longevity benefits. Ongoing research continues to explore these possibilities.

GLP-1 is an endogenous incretin hormone, with poor bioavailability as it is quickly degraded by DPP-4. It is not marketed directly, but analogs like exenatide and liraglutide are used for therapeutic purposes.
Hydrochlorothiazide is an effective diuretic frequently used to manage hypertension and edema.
Modafinil is a well-researched wakefulness-promoting agent that helps maintain alertness.
Arsole is primarily studied as a theoretical analog to pyrrole and is not a pharmaceutical compound. Limited data exists on its pharmacokinetics and safety.
DOG DOG DOG DOG DOG

Psilocin, the active metabolite of psilocybin, acts as a selective 5-HT₂A agonist that enhances neuroplasticity, shifts emotional bias toward positivity, and reduces stress-related rumination. By lowering inflammation, improving mood regulation, and fostering durable personality changes like openness, psilocin may indirectly promote resilience, healthy aging, and longevity.

Sesquiterpenes are complex natural compounds found in essential oils. They are located in tiny hair-like glands on leaves, flowers, and stems.
Sesquiterpenes deliver powerful biological benefits — they can inhibit pro-inflammatory pathways (such as NF-κB and COX-2) and exhibit antimicrobial, neuroprotective, and anticancer properties.
They also act as signaling molecules in plant–insect communication. Essential both in nature’s chemistry and in modern pharmacology

Pinealon is a synthetic tri-peptide discovered by world-renowned professor Vladimir Khavinson, with 4+ decades of studies on traumatic brain injury, neurodegenerative disease, stroke, radiation-resistance, stress resilience, & cognitive impairment.

It activates over 870 longevity-related genes and helps mitigate risk of APOE4 gene for Alzheimer's.  Reduced HR by 10-12 bpm in endurance athletes; 150% increase in learning speed; 25% increase in hippocampal neuron density across different studies

LSD (lysergic acid diethylamide) is a potent serotonergic psychedelic and probe of consciousness now emerging in geroscience. In C. elegans, LSD extended lifespan by ~25%, reduced age pigments, and mimicked caloric restriction via TOR modulation. Beyond the brain, it alters hormones, immune tone, and metabolism, with surveys linking psychedelic use to lower heart disease and diabetes. LSD may act as both a mind-expanding and lifespan-extending molecule.
Sildenafil (marketed as Viagra) has been the best treatment for erectile dysfunction. Recent studies showed a strong association between its use and increased lifespan in humans.
just a test
SMER28 - Rapamycin experiment

Paracetamol, also known as acetaminophen, is a common analgesic and antipyretic. It is extensively utilized due to its efficacy and safety profile when used appropriately. Its wide availability and affordability make it a staple in pain management.

A Neuro-Autophagy Longevity Research Stack
Spermidine + Psilocin + CBD  

NeuroTrio™ is a preclinical longevity research stack designed to explore whether targeting neural plasticity, neuroinflammation, and cellular cleanup simultaneously can improve lifespan and healthspan outcomes across model organisms. The NEUROTRIO token represents a prediction market on the strength, direction, and reproducibility of this hypothesis when tested in worms, flies, and mice.

Unlike stacks that focus primarily on metabolic or nutrient-sensing pathways, NeuroTrio™ is explicitly neuro-centric. It is built around the idea that aging of the nervous system—characterized by declining plasticity, chronic inflammation, and accumulation of damaged cellular components—may be a critical bottleneck for both lifespan and quality of life. NeuroTrio™ asks whether addressing these processes together can meaningfully shift aging trajectories in living systems.

NeuroTrio™ is not a supplement, or treatment,

ATPX™ is a next-gen cellular energy blend combining Urolithin A, D-Ribose, and ATP Disodium to support mitochondrial health, boost ATP production, and accelerate recovery. Engineered for performance, backed by science.

The most expensive liquid in the world is finally available commercially.

Made in the USA and FDA-listed with National Drug Code NDC 83627-901-01, Blue Scorpion® Pain & Inflammation Relief delivers a uniquely advanced approach to total-body inflammation relief. It is also the only venom-based TruShield-certified formula trusted for use by professional athletes and professional sports teams.

This homeopathic medicine harnesses the power of Blue Scorpion Venom through a patented polarization process, combined with Apis Mellifica and Rhus Tox, to offer safe, temporary relief from pain and inflammation.

Resveratrol is known for cell repair & anti-aging, Berberine helps with metabolism & blood sugar. Both are huge in the supplement world — but no one’s tested how they work together to boost longevity. Until now!

Catechins are powerful plant antioxidants that contribute to many of the health benefits associated with tea and plant-based diets. They belong to a larger group of plant compounds called flavonoids and are specifically a type of flavan-3-ol. 
Research has shown potential benefits including anti-inflammatory and anti-viral activities, and possible effects on conditions like diabetes and heart disease.

For enhancing male vitality, Tadalafil, L-Arginine, and Ginseng are effective options. Tadalafil is a reliable PDE5 inhibitor prescribed for erectile dysfunction by promoting blood flow. L-Arginine serves as a precursor to nitric oxide, improving vascular health. Ginseng, a traditional adaptogen, supports energy and stamina. These compounds are available either by prescription or over-the-counter, with established safety profiles.

Taurine (T), isolated 1827, was initially just bile, later recognized as the cellular efficiency molecule. It provides new energy and sharp concentration by stabilizing mitochondria and regulating electrolytes—the body's internal power grid.
Chloroquine (Q), synthesized 1934, was an antimalarial, later found to be a potent immunomodulator. Its true longevity breakthrough (c. 2040) involved lysosomal regulation, enhancing autophagy (cellular recycling). This deep cleaning provided cellular freedom from waste and powerful anti-oxidation.
The T/Q Epoch (c. 2050) revealed the crucial synergy: Q's intense cleaning placed metabolic strain; T provided the necessary power stabilization and energy support. This dual-action regimen maximized energy (T) while minimizing cellular damage (Q), solving the core paradox of aging and initiating true healthspan extension—a revolution built on sustained cellular vitality and cleanliness.

Penicillin G is a classic penicillin, widely recognized for its efficacy against many Gram-positive organisms. Its safety profile is well-established, though allergies are common. Its use is largely limited to injectable form due to poor oral bioavailability.
Glycine forms the cornerstone of this stack, synergizing well with NAC (as GlyNAC) to enhance glutathione levels, targeting oxidative stress and improving mitochondrial function.
The ultimate upper.

NR + Sulforaphane + Fisetin form a powerful, multi-pathway combo targeting key hallmarks of aging. It mimics traits seen in ultra-long-lived species (bowhead whale)
- NR boosts NAD⁺ levels, fueling DNA repair and activating SIR-2.1 for metabolic longevity.
- Sulforaphane activates stress-response pathways like DAF-16/FOXO and enhances antioxidant defense.
- Fisetin adds anti-inflammatory action and may support proteostasis and cellular cleanup.
It taps into deeply conserved longevity biology.

Pantethine is a derivative of vitamin B5 (pantothenic acid) that plays a role in coenzyme A (CoA) metabolism. Since CoA is essential for the citric acid cycle and fatty acid β-oxidation, pantethine contributes to energy metabolism. Pantethine has also been investigated in studies involving models of neurodegeneration with brain iron accumulation (NBIA), including cell and mouse models
Selegiline may have a minor lifespan-extending effect due to its symptom management and potential neuroprotection, but this is not definitively proven.
Phenethylamine, while naturally occurring in the human brain, can also be taken as a supplement, primarily noted for its mood-enhancing properties, though it can stimulate wakefulness.
Don't buy - this is a test
Dapoxetine is already approved for enhancing performance by delaying ejaculation. Its rapid onset makes it suitable for this indication.

Diclofenac Sodium is a well-established non-steroidal anti-inflammatory drug (NSAID) with extensive clinical data supporting its pharmacological profile, including molecular characteristics, mechanism, and safety.

NeuroFerm™ is a preclinical longevity research stack designed to test whether combining neural plasticity signaling, metabolic regulation, and inflammation control can produce measurable lifespan and healthspan benefits across model organisms. The NEUROFERM token represents a prediction market on the strength, direction, and reproducibility of this hypothesis when evaluated in worms, flies, and mice.

While many longevity strategies focus narrowly on nutrient sensing or cellular cleanup, NeuroFerm™ explores a hybrid metabolic-neuro paradigm. The core idea is that aging is driven not only by accumulated cellular damage, but also by declining metabolic flexibility and impaired stress response—processes that strongly influence brain function and systemic resilience. NeuroFerm™ asks whether engaging these axes together reveals aging signals that are invisible to single-pathway interventions.

NeuroFerm™ is not a supplement, therapy, or clinical recommendation. It is a discovery

Semax is a synthetic peptide drug developed in Russia and classified as a nootropic and neuroprotective agent. It was originally derived from a fragment of the adrenocorticotropic hormone (ACTH), but unlike ACTH, Semax does not exhibit hormonal activity. It is primarily used for its cognitive-enhancing, neuroregenerative, and neuroprotective properties.

Variotin is a compound derived from a eukaryotic extract (Aspergillus unguis IV17-109). Variotin exhibited anti-inflammatory efficacy, inhibiting 50% of interleukin-6 (IL-6) biological activity at a concentration of 20.0 µM. Experiments with this compound will be conducted on mice

Mephedrone (4-MMC, “Meow Meow”) is a synthetic stimulantempathogen in the cathinone family. It’s lab-made but chemically related to cathinone, the natural stimulant in the khat plant. Effects: energy, euphoria, talkativeness. Risks: rapid heart rate, overheating, anxiety, insomnia, and harsh comedowns; dose/purity are unpredictable. Illegal in many countries and has no approved medical use.

$UNICORNS, its our Trojan horse for Quinine, derived from the bark of the Cinchona tree has been used for centuries, primarily to treat malaria and to reduce inflammation. Its effects on inflammation are significant, as chronic low-grade inflammation, or "inflammaging," is linked to a range of age-related diseases such as cardiovascular disease, neurodegeneration, and autoimmune disorders. Quinine’s anti-inflammatory effects could mitigate this process, slowing down the biological aging process.
test token don't buy

For weight loss, these compounds—Orlistat, Liraglutide, and Phentermine—offer scientifically backed paths to slimming down. Each brings its own charm, whether it's blocking fat absorption or tickling your appetite control centers. Yet, they also carry potential side effects that require a discerning eye. These drugs are available by prescription only, ensuring that their dance with your metabolism is a well-choreographed affair rather than a reckless fling.

Thioflavin T (ThT) is a fluorescent dye known for binding amyloid aggregates, but at low concentrations, it has shown remarkable lifespan extension effects in C. elegans. Previous studies demonstrated that ThT enhances proteostasis and stress resistance by stabilizing protein folding, reducing age-related toxicity. 
ThT is inexpensive, safe at research doses, and represents a unique, underexplored path for longevity research targeting protein homeostasis.

RAPTIDE combines the foundational mTOR-blocking power of Rapamycin with two elite strategic partners. Artemisinin acts as a hormetic boot camp, training cellular defenses to peak readiness, while Ginsenoside Rh2 serves as a precision clean-up crew, purging the chronic, low-grade inflammation that drives the aging process. This is a coordinated assault: apply the brakes, build the shield, and clear the damage for a profound extension of healthspan.
Projected median lifespan extension in C. elegan
Acarbose's inhibitory activity on alpha-glucosidase enzymes is well-documented. The low bioavailability and negative logP align with its minimal systemic absorption, focusing its effect on the gut.
Psilocybin is a well-known compound with potential therapeutic applications in psychiatric disorders. It acts primarily on serotonin receptors, which are associated with mood regulation.

Rapamycin and Trametinib present a duo where the mTOR and MEK pathways are intriguingly inhibited. This combination could expand therapeutic avenues, especially in oncology. By including Cobimetinib, another MEK inhibitor, we add an ensemble of precision targeting, each member enhancing therapeutic harmony. All compounds are commercially available, offering access and practical utility in treatment regimens.

RapaMeta Eternum™
research‑grade combo concept uniting mTOR inhibition and AMPK modulation for systemic longevity biology.

Mechanistic concept
RapaMeta Eternum™ is positioned as a dual‑axis longevity stack that integrates:
Rapamycin, a well‑validated inhibitor of mTORC1 that extends lifespan and delays multiple age‑related pathologies in mice, even when started late in life.

Metformin, a front‑line metabolic therapeutic that reduces hepatic gluconeogenesis, improves peripheral insulin sensitivity, and modestly activates AMPK‑linked pathways.

In genetically heterogeneous HET3 mice, chronic dietary rapamycin (14 ppm encapsulated) extended lifespan but induced glucose intolerance and mild insulin resistance, particularly in males. Metformin at 0.1% dietary inclusion alone did not meaningfully change glucose tolerance, yet in combination it reshaped the metabolic phenotype in a sex‑specific way. RapaMeta Eternum™ encodes this synergy: longevity signaling via mTORC1 ‑linked geroprotecti

MER11‑SomaEon takes its inspiration from Soma Ras and the lunar cycle, framing metabolism and circadian timing as intertwined levers for extended healthspan.
​
Within the MER11 Mythos Longevity Series, SomaEon is defined as the metabolic and circadian longevity modulator: a scaffold class reserved for agents that reshape fuel handling, inflammatory tone, and daily transcriptional rhythms without tipping into harsh, pro‑apoptotic stress.
​

Mechanistic role and target profile
Conceptually, SomaEon is designed to be a MER11‑linked modulator of metabolic and chrono‑biological networks, with NMR‑015 NanoMER2—an LNP‑delivered MER11 silencer for metabolic syndrome—highlighted as a prime fit.
​
In this framing, SomaEon agents would tune MER11‑associated retroelement activity in liver, adipose, muscle, and central pacemaker tissues so that clock‑controlled genes, insulin signaling, lipid handling, and inflammatory programs shift toward youthful, resilient set points.
​

Apigenin, found in chamomile and celery, flirts with the concept of health maintenance through its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Though understudied, it holds potential intrigue for mitigating age-related decline through subtle modulation of cellular pathways.
Methocarbamol works as a general CNS depressant, offering effective relief for muscle spasticity with a generally mild side effect profile.
Elamipretide has a similar structure and therapeutic approach as SS-31, with confirmed clinical trial data supporting its bioavailability, safety, and efficacy.

Fuel of Life, Power of Chains. $DEX is inspired by D-(+)-Glucose, the natural energy source for all living cells. Just as glucose powers the body, $DEX aims to energize the decentralized world with unstoppable vitality. Clean, safe, and essential. $DEX is the tokenized spark of life itself.
Dasatinib is a well-established BCR-ABL tyrosine kinase inhibitor with extensive clinical use in hematological cancers.
Doxorubicin is a well-studied and widely used chemotherapeutic agent that effectively targets rapidly dividing cancer cells by disrupting DNA processes.

Morphine, the classic pain whisperer, dances with mu-opioid receptors, orchestrating a symphony of analgesia. Despite its prowess, it carries the shadow of dependency and requires a gentle yet firm hand in its administration.

In the Perennial Forest dwells Allyxir, a primordial Pumpscienemon. Resembling armored garlic, this guardian embodies the fight against aging.

Born from the pure essence of Diallyl Trisulfide (DATS), its very structure mimics DATS's sulfur bonds (S-S-S), channeling energy to counter cellular decay. Allyxir’s powers are purely biochemical:

Vital Aura: Emits compounds that boost natural antioxidants (like glutathione) to fight the free radicals that cause aging.

Rebirth Song: A vibration that activates telomerase, repairing telomeres to slow genetic aging.

Purifying Pulse: Generates an energy wave that detoxifies the body, easing oxidative stress.

Blood Shield: Enhances cardiovascular health, improving circulation and vessel elasticity.

Allyxir is not a giver of immortality, but a Guardian of Balance. It appears only to the pure of heart, a living symbol of DATS's power and the eternal quest for vitality.

Combining Minocycline, Rapamycin, and Metformin offers the best theoretical chance for maximizing lifespan extension by targeting FOXO (stress resistance), mTOR (autophagy), and AMPK (metabolism). This trio leverages complementary mechanisms with strong preclinical evidence in model organisms and some human data for age-related benefits.
SENO is the Fisetin based senolytic compound token focused on cellular cleanup, regeneration, and longevity science.
Armodafinil is the R-enantiomer of modafinil, offering similar benefits with potentially prolonged action.

Rapamycin inhibits the mTOR (mechanistic target of rapamycin) pathway, which is crucial for regulating cellular growth, metabolism, and aging. $Rapalogs are modified versions of Rapamycin aimed at reducing side effects while maintaining or enhancing its longevity benefits. adding high antioxidant compound will accelerate the research.

Dimethyltryptamine (DMT) is a naturally occurring psychedelic compound. It is known for its potent effects and rapid onset, making it a subject of interest in psychedelic research. Its legal status varies and it is largely available for research rather than therapeutic use.

Resveratrol is a natural compound found in the skins of grapes and in red wine. What makes it so intriguing is its ability to activate sirtuins—proteins that play a key role in cellular health and longevity. Think of it as a little boost for your cells, similar to the benefits of calorie restriction. In studies on yeast and mice, resveratrol has shown potential to extend lifespan and improve healthspan. Early human studies are exploring its potential to support heart health and slow aging.

AMPK:  longevity switch, how fasting, exercise, and key drugs trigger repair and cellular cleanup.
AICAR is a direct activator: unlike indirect hacks.
Proven in mice: boosts endurance, brain function, insulin sensitivity, and protects against obesity-driven damage.
Untapped in humans: early disease-targeted trials showed safety and some efficacy. 
Yet AICAR has never been tested for aging or healthspan neither in animals nor in humans.

A DeSci token inspired by the synergy of Gossypol & (+)-Catechin Hydrate, two powerful bio-compounds with deep biochemical and therapeutic significance. Pioneering decentralized research, funding scientific discovery, and taking innovation on-chain. From the lab to the chain.

Dasatinib and Quercetin (D+Q) combination therapy, ticker $DQ. This senolytic cocktail has shown significant potential in preclinical studies, Dasatinib is a tyrosine kinase inhibitor, and Quercetin is a flavonoid with antioxidant properties. Together, they act as senolytics, targeting and eliminating senescent cells, which accumulate with age and contribute to aging and age-related diseases.  Studies in mice have demonstrated that D+Q can clear senescent cells, improve physical function.

Withaferin A is a bioactive compound found in Ashwagandha (Withania somnifera). It shows significant anti-inflammatory and anticancer properties by inhibiting NF-kB signaling, which is key in regulating immune responses. It is commercially available, aligning with your interest in Ashwagandha.

Berberine ignites your metabolism. Biotin fuels transactions for cellular efficiency checks. Known for their individual health benefits, BXB is being tested for synergy in improving longevity for the first time! Bullish on improving health from the inside out.

Minocycline, an FDA-approved antibiotic, has been known to boost C. elegans lifespan by 35-48% (50 µM), outpacing many anti-aging compounds. It targets ribosomes, the cell’s protein factories, to reduce harmful protein aggregation, enhancing proteostasis and stress resilience. This novel mechanism differs from rapamycin or NAD+ boosters, offering a fresh longevity approach. Safe at 100-200 mg/day in humans, minocycline’s high efficacy makes it a prime candidate for advancing anti-aging research.
Precursor to NAD⁺ (energy cofactor), from ribose salvage pathways. Fuels mitochondria staking and repair—like auto-compounding yield. Bullish for sustained bioenergetic ROI.
Citrus-derived antioxidant plus microbe-made B12. Shields cells from oxidation and ramps up red-cell production—paired stability protocol for defense and energy transfer. Bullish for immunity.
Berberine (goldenseal) and quercetin (onion/apple). Balance blood sugar and calm inflammation—think of a dual-token pool that auto-stabilizes your metabolic ledger. Bullish for steady energy.

Astragaloside IV is a bioactive compound extracted from Astragalus membranaceus, known for its telomere-protecting, antioxidant, and anti-inflammatory properties that may slow cellular aging and support neuroprotection. As a drug hunter with over a decade of experience in pharmaceutical R&D, I propose Astragaloside IV as a promising longevity candidate worthy of deeper exploration through experiments performed through Pump.science.

Rifampicin is traditionally known as an antibiotic, but it’s been gaining attention for its surprising effects on aging. In tiny organisms like C. elegans (a model organism often used in aging research), Rifampicin has been shown to activate the cell’s natural defense mechanisms against stress and damage. Imagine it as a sort of "cellular coach," encouraging cells to stay healthy and resilient by protecting against harmful oxidative stress and maintaining the quality of proteins within the cell. These protective effects help the worms live longer and healthier lives. While it’s still early days, and we don’t yet know if Rifampicin can do the same in humans, its ability to promote cellular health makes it an exciting area of research in the quest for anti-aging therapies​.
From coffee beans and tea leaves. Blocks fatigue signals to spike alertness—your real-time oracle feed for instant performance gains. Bullish for tactical focus.
From Panax ginseng root. Boosts brain-cell signals and energy output—like giving your node extra CPU to process transactions faster. Bullish for sharper focus and fatigue resistance.

My role is to help you not just live, but to thrive—extending your healthspan and optimizing your body's innate ability to stay young. I am the science behind a longer, more vibrant life.
Triple hit vs multiple causes of aging. C to maintain telomeres, reduce inflammation, promote autophagy. T to improve blood flow, protec arteries. H to clean up sterols and cell membranes.

Spermidine is a naturally occurring molecule that activates autophagy — the body’s cellular recycling system. It clears out damaged proteins, supports mitochondrial health, and slows aging at the cellular level. Research shows spermidine extends lifespan in yeast, flies, and mice, with benefits including improved memory, cardiovascular health, and immune resilience. $SPERMN brings spermidine on-chain. Every trade fuels real-time experiments in worms, flies & mice via Pump.Science.

Cannabidiol (CBD) is a non-psychoactive compound (cannabinoid), which is derived from Cannabis sativa plants. 
CBD consistently extended lifespan in nematodes (C. elegans) across multiple studies, with increases ranging from 7.7% to 40% depending on the concentration used. It also increases lifespan in fruit flies & rats. 
It works by inducing autophagy (cellular cleanup) through sirtuin pathways, and enhances antioxidant systems & AMPK signalling to offset cellular senescence.

Omipalisib (GSK2126458). More than 2x the lifespan extension in worms than the most robustly validated longevity compound to date which is Rapamycin. Omipalisib was recently shown to be the best lifespan-enhancing compound in the largest screen of mTOR modulators in worms. Inhibits the mTOR pathway, a strategy shown to extend lifespan across every species tested including worm, flies, mice and primates. Discovered in 2024  by Epiterna and Rapamycin Longevity Lab in collab with Ora Biomedical.
